description: Audit category definitions used by context-audit for structured evaluation.

categories.name: Level Appropriateness
categories.description: Whether context complexity matches project complexity.

under_documented: Multiple distinct layers but only a minimal AGENTS.md
More than 10 top-level directories with no project structure section
Auth packages present but no auth section
Multiple package manager configs (monorepo) but no cascading files
More than 3 contributors (git log) but no working style documentation
over_engineered: Context directory with 5+ files for a project with fewer than 20 source files
Subdirectory AGENTS.md files in a project with a flat structure
Cascading setup for a single-person, single-language project
Multiple context files that mostly repeat the same information

categories.name: Section Completeness
categories.description: Whether required sections are present for the current context level.

full: Auth and permissions (if auth packages present)
cascading: Root AGENTS.md as entry point (under ~60 lines)
Context directory with system overview and architecture decisions
Root guidance stays durable across long sessions
Subdirectory AGENTS.md only where distinct patterns exist

categories.name: Format and Conventions
checks: AGENTS.md exists as primary file
CLAUDE.md is symlink to AGENTS.md (not duplicate)
MEMORY.md treated as optional working memory if present
Do NOT entries are specific and actionable
Commands match actual executable scripts
No bracket placeholders remaining in non-template files
Consistent markdown headings (H2 for main sections)

categories.name: Structural Issues
categories.description: Common structural problems in context files.
checks: Duplicate subdirectory files (repeat root content)
Empty context files (headings only, no content)
Cascading contradictions (test runner, linter, package manager, framework version, import style, Do NOT boundaries)
Missing coverage for directories with distinct patterns
Orphaned references to non-existent files or directories
Overloaded root guidance (volatile migration notes, sprint state)
Misused MEMORY.md (duplicates AGENTS.md or permanent policy)

categories.name: Content Quality
checks: Vague boundaries (Do NOT entries not actionable enough)
Stale framework references (tech stack vs installed deps)
Missing symlink (CLAUDE.md is regular file or missing)
Overly long root file (exceeds ~150 lines)
Missing integration map (2+ service client packages but no mapping)
Missing long-session design (enough complexity for context/ split but root carries everything)

categories.name: MCP Tool Notes Cross-Reference
categories.description: Whether MCP servers are configured but undocumented.
checks: Scan project-level MCP configs (.mcp.json, .codex/config.toml, .gemini/settings.json, .vscode/mcp.json, .cursor/mcp.json)
Flag if MCP config found but no MCP Tool Notes section in AGENTS.md
Flag if MCP Tool Notes exists but is still bracket placeholders
Skip silently if no MCP configs found

categories.name: Trust Boundary Coverage
categories.description: Whether operator-owned automation surfaces are acknowledged.
checks: Scan for .mcp.json, .claude/settings.json, .claude/hooks/, .codex/config.toml, .cursor/, hook scripts
Flag if present but root context lacks trust-boundary guidance
Flag if context mentions MCP/hooks only as tools, not as trust surfaces
Flag if root guidance implies agents may freely edit operator-owned automation

categories.name: Command Output Optimization
categories.description: Whether CLI commands could produce less output.
structural_checks: Commands section exists but no Command Output Notes (for full level or higher)
Command Output Notes exists but is still bracket placeholders
